profiles.compatibility: All 143 canonical union tools; exact duplicate registrations and three Workflow-owned self-record front doors removed.
profiles.full: Canonical local surface; seven redundant compatibility front doors and two absorbed tools hidden.
profiles.default: Full minus manual attach-lock acquire/release; adds one catalog tool.

redundant_front_doors.retry_click: browser_click(..., retry=clamp(max_attempts, 1, 3), retry_delay_ms=retry_delay_ms); for more than 3 attempts, use explicitly bounded repeated browser_click steps in hands_script or Workflow
redundant_front_doors.find_and_click: optional uia_focus_window(title=window_title); hands_click(target=text) for left/right; for middle or exact coordinate control use hands_find(target=text) then uia_click(x, y, button, double_click); scan/focus windows explicitly to preserve legacy cross-window search
redundant_front_doors.read_screen_text: hands_capture(target=window_title or "screen", window_title=window_title, ocr=true), or vision_screenshot_ocr for full-screen OCR
redundant_front_doors.type_into_window: uia_focus_window(title); wait delay_ms; optional uia_click(x=click_x, y=click_y); uia_type(text)
redundant_front_doors.window_screenshot: behind=true: vision_screenshot_hidden_window(title, save_path, ocr); behind=false: hands_capture(target=title, window_title=title, save_path=save_path, ocr=ocr)

folded.vision_ocr_fast: vision_ocr with automatic cache and backend metadata

workflow_owned.hands_self_record_lookup: no exact Workflow equivalent; use explicit flow selection and current-site validation

tools.name: browser_a11y_find
tools.ability: Find nodes in the cached/refreshed accessibility snapshot by role and name.

tools.name: browser_a11y_snapshot
tools.ability: Build an accessibility-oriented DOM snapshot with ref IDs usable by click(a11y_ref=...).

tools.name: browser_agent
tools.ability: DOM-based browser agent. Executes multi-step workflows using DOM heuristics (fuzzy text matching, no vision). Returns step-by-step narrative log. On failure, saves screenshot and returns available DOM elements.

tools.name: browser_attach
tools.ability: Attach to existing Chrome with remote debugging. Start Chrome with --remote-debugging-port=9222

tools.name: browser_back
tools.ability: Go back in history

tools.name: browser_batch
tools.ability: Execute multiple browser actions in sequence. Each action calls the same internal function as the individual tool. Stops on first error unless continue_on_error=true. Returns array of results.

tools.name: browser_bulk_extract
tools.ability: Extract text from multiple URLs in sequence. Uses smart_browse auto-escalation for each URL.

tools.name: browser_click
tools.ability: Click element by CSS selector, XPath, coordinates, or fuzzy text match. Supports automatic retry on transient failures via the `retry`/`retry_delay_ms` options subsumes the legacy `retry_click` combo tool.

tools.name: browser_close
tools.ability: Close browser

tools.name: browser_close_tab
tools.ability: Close a browser tab by index.

tools.name: browser_context_create
tools.ability: Create a named browser context with isolated page(s). Each context has separate cookies, storage, and DOM state.

tools.name: browser_context_destroy
tools.ability: Destroy a named context and close all its pages

tools.name: browser_context_list
tools.ability: List all browser contexts and their page counts

tools.name: browser_context_switch
tools.ability: Switch to a named browser context. Current context is saved and can be switched back to.

tools.name: browser_cookies
tools.ability: Manage cookies: get, set, clear

tools.name: browser_crawl
tools.ability: Polite recursive crawler: fetches a site from a start URL, honoring robots.txt, per-domain rate limits, and an honest user-agent. Bounded by max_depth/max_pages/timeout. Returns clean text per page. Set CPC_CRAWLER_PROXY to egress through a non-home IP.

tools.name: browser_debug_launch
tools.ability: Launch YOUR Chrome with debug port enabled. Keeps your logins/sessions. Waits for CDP to be ready before returning (configurable). Then use browser_attach to connect.

tools.name: browser_eval
tools.ability: Execute JavaScript

tools.name: browser_evaluate
tools.ability: Evaluate a web artifact. With spec: runs test steps with assertions. Without spec: auto-discovery mode checks page load, console errors, broken images, unlabeled inputs, link validity, a11y summary, and page metrics. Returns structured pass/fail/warning with screenshot evidence.

tools.name: browser_exists
tools.ability: Check if element exists

tools.name: browser_extract_content
tools.ability: Extract clean article/main content from URL, stripping nav, ads, and boilerplate

tools.name: browser_fill_form
tools.ability: Fill form fields by name

tools.name: browser_focus
tools.ability: Focus element

tools.name: browser_forward
tools.ability: Go forward in history

tools.name: browser_get_all_network
tools.ability: Get ALL network activity by merging route-based logs (browser_get_network_log) and Performance API logs (browser_get_performance_log). Each entry includes a 'source' field ('route' or 'performance'). Provides the most complete picture of network requests.

tools.name: browser_get_bounds
tools.ability: Get element position and size

tools.name: browser_get_clickables
tools.ability: Get all clickable elements with coordinates (for vision)

tools.name: browser_get_forms
tools.ability: Get all forms on page with their fields

tools.name: browser_get_html
tools.ability: Get page or element HTML

tools.name: browser_get_metrics
tools.ability: Get page metrics (scroll position, dimensions)

tools.name: browser_get_network_log
tools.ability: Get intercepted/logged network requests captured by routes

tools.name: browser_get_performance_log
tools.ability: Get recent network requests from the browser via performance.getEntriesByType('resource'). Returns URL, type, duration, and size for each request. Lightweight no CDP hooks needed, works with any page. Clears entries after reading so subsequent calls return only new requests.

tools.name: browser_get_text
tools.ability: Get text content of element

tools.name: browser_get_url
tools.ability: Get current page URL

tools.name: browser_hover
tools.ability: Hover over element

tools.name: browser_http_scrape
tools.ability: Simple HTTP GET, strip HTML, return text. Fast fallback when JS rendering not needed. No browser spin-up.

tools.name: browser_iframe_extract
tools.ability: Extract content from iframes on current page. Auto-detects same-origin (reads DOM) vs cross-origin (screenshots + OCR).

tools.name: browser_inject_script
tools.ability: Inject and execute JavaScript

tools.name: browser_js_extract
tools.ability: Spawn Node.js to extract content from a URL without Chrome. Supports linkedom or jsdom engines.

tools.name: browser_launch
tools.ability: Launch browser. headless=true for no window, profile_path for persistent session

tools.name: browser_learn_api
tools.ability: Analyze captured network traffic to discover API endpoints. Call this AFTER interacting with a page to extract the APIs it uses. Returns structured endpoint patterns (URL, method, headers, body template) that can be stored and replayed via direct HTTP calls.

tools.name: browser_list_tab
tools.ability: List all open browser tabs with their URLs and titles.

tools.name: browser_map
tools.ability: Discover URLs on a site without extracting content. Reads sitemap.xml (incl. sitemap indexes) and supplements with a shallow link crawl. Returns a deduped URL list.

tools.name: browser_navigate
tools.ability: Navigate to URL

tools.name: browser_new_tab
tools.ability: Open new tab

tools.name: browser_page_capture
tools.ability: Capture the current page as image, DOM dump, scroll-collected dump, or saved artifact bundle.

tools.name: browser_page_dump
tools.ability: Alias for page_capture(mode='dom' or mode='bundle'): structured page data with optional saved artifacts.

tools.name: browser_plan
tools.ability: Analyze a task and return its ingredients: what tools are needed, which depend on each other, and whether breadcrumbing is warranted.

tools.name: browser_press
tools.ability: Press keyboard key

tools.name: browser_reload
tools.ability: Reload current page

tools.name: browser_route
tools.ability: Add a network interception route. Intercepts fetch() and XHR requests matching the URL pattern.

tools.name: browser_route_clear
tools.ability: Disable all network interception and restore normal fetch/XHR

tools.name: browser_route_list
tools.ability: List all active network interception routes

tools.name: browser_route_remove
tools.ability: Remove a network interception route by pattern

tools.name: browser_screenshot
tools.ability: Take screenshot. Returns base64 JPEG unless save_path provided (then saves to file, returns path)

tools.name: browser_screenshot_burst
tools.ability: Rapid screenshots for state tracking

tools.name: browser_script
tools.ability: Execute a batch of browser steps with variable substitution. Steps run sequentially; {{var}} placeholders replaced from vars map. On failure, returns partial results + screenshot.

tools.name: browser_scroll
tools.ability: Scroll page

tools.name: browser_scroll_collect
tools.ability: Scroll the active page until lazy-loaded content stops expanding, then extract content.

tools.name: browser_select
tools.ability: Select dropdown option

tools.name: browser_smart_browse
tools.ability: Try HTTP scrape first, then escalate to linkedom, then jsdom, and finally flag when Chrome rendering is needed.

tools.name: browser_smart_navigate
tools.ability: Launch or attach if needed, navigate, wait, and optionally verify page state with evidence.

tools.name: browser_status
tools.ability: Get browser status

tools.name: browser_submit_form
tools.ability: Submit a form

tools.name: browser_switch_tab
tools.ability: Switch to a different browser tab by index or URL match.

tools.name: browser_trace_save
tools.ability: Stop trace recording and save to a JSON file

tools.name: browser_trace_start
tools.ability: Start recording a browser trace. Captures navigation, clicks, resource loading, paint timing, and URL changes.

tools.name: browser_trace_stop
tools.ability: Stop trace recording and return all captured entries as JSON

tools.name: browser_type
tools.ability: Type text into element by selector, XPath, or fuzzy text match

tools.name: browser_verify_state
tools.ability: Verify page state with DOM checks first and optional screenshot/OCR fallback.

tools.name: browser_verify_visual
tools.ability: Take screenshot and verify expected text is present via OCR. Returns pass/fail with OCR text.

tools.name: browser_wait_for
tools.ability: Wait for element by CSS selector or XPath. condition: 'visible' (default, checks CSS visibility), 'exists' (DOM only)

tools.name: browser_wait_idle
tools.ability: Wait for network idle

tools.name: browser_wait_stable
tools.ability: Wait for visual stability by comparing consecutive screenshot file sizes.

tools.name: drag
tools.ability: Mouse drag from one point to another. Useful for moving windows, resizing, selecting text.

tools.name: element_drag
tools.ability: Drag from one CSS-selector element to another, or from a selector to an offset. Resolves element positions via browser get_bounds, then performs a smooth mouse drag. Requires browser to be launched.

tools.name: file_upload
tools.ability: Upload file(s) to an input[type=file] element. Sets files via DataTransfer API and triggers change event. Works in headless mode.

tools.name: find_and_click
tools.ability: DEPRECATED 2026-05-15 use hands_click (offset_x/offset_y subsume the find_and_click offset behavior; rungs 6+7 cover OCR + UIA fallback). Find text on screen via OCR, then click its location via UIA. Combines vision + UIA in one call. When window_title is provided, focuses that window first. When text isn't found on the visible screen, automatically tries focusing other windows to find it.
tools.replacement: optional uia_focus_window(title=window_title); hands_click(target=text) for left/right; for middle or exact coordinate control use hands_find(target=text) then uia_click(x, y, button, double_click); scan/focus windows explicitly to preserve legacy cross-window search

tools.name: read_screen_text
tools.ability: DEPRECATED 2026-05-15 use hands_capture (accepts optional window_title to focus a named window before OCR) or vision_screenshot_ocr for screen-only OCR. Take a screenshot and return all text via OCR. Optionally target a specific window by title.
tools.replacement: hands_capture(target=window_title or "screen", window_title=window_title, ocr=true), or vision_screenshot_ocr for full-screen OCR

tools.name: retry_click
tools.ability: DEPRECATED 2026-05-15 use browser_click with the new retry / retry_delay_ms options (same behavior, fewer hops). Robust click with automatic retry. If the click fails (element not found, stale, not clickable), waits and retries up to max_attempts times. Supports same args as browser_click (selector, match_text, x, y).
tools.replacement: browser_click(..., retry=clamp(max_attempts, 1, 3), retry_delay_ms=retry_delay_ms); for more than 3 attempts, use explicitly bounded repeated browser_click steps in hands_script or Workflow

tools.name: status
tools.ability: Get status of all Hands subsystems: browser connection, UIA availability, vision capabilities.

tools.name: type_into_window
tools.ability: DEPRECATED 2026-05-15 use hands_type (adds focus verification, chunked typing for >100 chars, fast_set, and sensitive-field detection). Focus a window by title, optionally click at coordinates, then type text. Combines focus + click + type in one call.
tools.replacement: uia_focus_window(title); wait delay_ms; optional uia_click(x=click_x, y=click_y); uia_type(text)

tools.name: wait_for_visual
tools.ability: Poll the screen until specific text appears (via OCR) or a template image is found. Returns when found or times out.

tools.name: window_screenshot
tools.ability: PARTIALLY DEPRECATED 2026-05-15 for default (behind=false) capture, prefer `vision_screenshot` (focus the window yourself if needed, then screenshot). For obscured/background windows, prefer the new `vision_screenshot_hidden_window` tool (PrintWindow API, always-on). This combined tool remains registered for backward compatibility. Focus a window by title and take a screenshot of it. Returns OCR text + saves image. With behind=true, captures the window content even if it's obscured by other windows (uses Win32 PrintWindow API).
tools.replacement: behind=true: vision_screenshot_hidden_window(title, save_path, ocr); behind=false: hands_capture(target=title, window_title=title, save_path=save_path, ocr=ocr)

tools.name: hands_app_action
tools.ability: RECOMMENDED: Window management open, close, focus, minimize, maximize, restore, snap. Handles save dialogs on close (Auto/Save/Discard/Ask). Multi-monitor aware with monitor stickiness. Post-action verification included. Use instead of manual uia_app_launch + uia_focus_window + uia_window_state + uia_key_press chains.

tools.name: hands_attach_lock_acquire
tools.ability: Acquire a cross-process file lock for a Chrome debug port before browser_attach.

tools.name: hands_attach_lock_release
tools.ability: Release a previously acquired Chrome attach lock.

tools.name: hands_attach_lock_status
tools.ability: Inspect attach locks, owner PIDs, ages, and current-process ownership.

tools.name: hands_capture
tools.ability: RECOMMENDED: Screenshot a target with optional OCR text verification. Routes to browser, window, or full-screen capture automatically. Multi-monitor aware. Optional `window_title` focuses a named window via UIA before any capture (orthogonal to `target`) subsumes the legacy `read_screen_text` combo tool. Use instead of browser_screenshot + vision_ocr chains.

tools.name: hands_click
tools.ability: RECOMMENDED: Click any visible element across browser and desktop. Auto-escalates: a11y ref fuzzy text CSS coordinates UIA OCR. Tags reversibility (Reversible \| RequiresConfirmation \| Destructive) based on target text. When `offset_x`/`offset_y` are set, every rung resolves the element's bounding box and clicks at `center + offset` via coord-click subsumes the legacy `find_and_click` combo tool. Use instead of browser_click / uia_click / find_and_click.

tools.name: hands_fill_form
tools.ability: RECOMMENDED: Fill an entire form with multiple fields. Pre-scans form structure, matches labels to inputs, fills each field with appropriate strategy (type, select, checkbox toggle). Tracks per-field success/failure. Optional auto-submit with spatial proximity tiebreaker for submit button. Use instead of manual hands_click + hands_type sequences.

tools.name: hands_find
tools.ability: RECOMMENDED: Find any element across browser and desktop. 6-rung ladder: a11y text a11y role clickables UIA OCR template. Returns ref, coords, or text match with confidence. Use return_type='ref' for elements you'll interact with next. Use instead of browser_a11y_find / uia_find / vision_ocr search chains.

tools.name: hands_health
tools.ability: Diagnostic health check for the hands server. Returns cpc-paths path resolution status (Volumes, install, backups) plus browser, vision, and UIA subsystem probe results.

tools.name: hands_login_recovery
tools.ability: RECOMMENDED: 5-stage login recovery pipeline. Navigates to URL, checks if already logged in, fills credentials, handles 2FA, discovers OAuth options, checks Remember Me, and verifies success. Built on hands_script returns full step-by-step results. Provide credentials or credential_name for auto-fill, totp_name for auto-2FA.

tools.name: hands_navigate
tools.ability: RECOMMENDED: Navigate browser to a URL and wait for interaction-ready state. Auto-launches browser if not running. Records owning monitor for multi-monitor awareness. Always reversible (back button). Use instead of browser_launch + browser_navigate + browser_wait_for chains.

tools.name: hands_network_poll
tools.ability: Return only network events newer than a subscription cursor.

tools.name: hands_network_subscribe
tools.ability: Create a filtered, cursor-based subscription over browser network events.

tools.name: hands_network_subscriptions
tools.ability: List active network subscriptions and their cursors/statistics.

tools.name: hands_network_unsubscribe
tools.ability: Remove a network-event subscription.

tools.name: hands_plugin_call
tools.ability: Invoke a tool exposed by a loaded Hands plugin.

tools.name: hands_plugin_list
tools.ability: List loaded Hands plugins, ABI versions, and exposed tools.

tools.name: hands_plugin_load
tools.ability: Load and validate a native Hands plugin library.

tools.name: hands_plugin_unload
tools.ability: Shut down and unload a Hands plugin.

tools.name: hands_read_page
tools.ability: RECOMMENDED: Fetch readable content from any URL. Auto-escalates HTTP scrape Node.js rendering Chrome as needed no manual browser setup required. Use instead of browser_http_scrape / browser_smart_browse / browser_navigate + browser_extract_content chains.

tools.name: hands_scan_qr
tools.ability: Scan a QR code from screen or browser, decode it, and validate otpauth:// URI format. Returns the decoded URI for registration via workflow:totp_register_from_uri. Supports full-screen capture or browser-only capture with optional region cropping.

tools.name: hands_script
tools.ability: RECOMMENDED: Execute a multi-step automation script. Chains meta-tool calls with {{var}} substitution, output capture, per-step error handling (stop/skip/retry), and per-step + overall timeouts. Use for login flows, form wizards, multi-page workflows. Use instead of manual meta-tool call sequences.

tools.name: hands_summarize_run
tools.ability: Summarize Hands run telemetry, latency, success rate, failures, and changed files.

tools.name: hands_type
tools.ability: RECOMMENDED: Type text into any input field across browser and desktop. Finds element, verifies focus, clears existing content, types with chunked input for long strings. Sensitive fields (password, phone) always use keystroke simulation. Use instead of browser_type / uia_type / type_into_window.

tools.name: hands_verify
tools.ability: RECOMMENDED: Verify page state with polling, stabilization, and multi-rung detection. 5-rung ladder: DOM text a11y snapshot element query OCR UIA. Supports natural language ('shows Welcome'), structured ({text: '...'}), or named templates (verify_page_loaded). Use instead of manual browser_eval + screenshot + OCR chains for post-action verification.

tools.name: hands_verify_expectations
tools.ability: Run multiple post-flow assertions with polling, predicates, and tolerance.

tools.name: vision_analyze
tools.ability: [Vision] Analyze image with Claude Vision API. Uses tokens but provides intelligent analysis.

tools.name: vision_cache_stats
tools.ability: Inspect or reset vision-cache entries, hit rate, misses, evictions, and TTL.

tools.name: vision_check_user_input
tools.ability: [Vision] Screenshot and OCR bottom of screen to detect if user typed during tool execution.

tools.name: vision_diff
tools.ability: [Vision] Compare two images and return difference percentage. Use 'screen' as image_b to compare against current screen.

tools.name: vision_find_template
tools.ability: [Vision] Find a template image within the screen or another image. Returns best match location and confidence.

tools.name: vision_load_image
tools.ability: [Vision] Load image as base64 for direct vision analysis in chat.

tools.name: vision_ocr
tools.ability: [Vision] Extract text from an image using Windows OCR. Local processing, no tokens used.

tools.name: vision_ocr_capabilities
tools.ability: Inspect current/planned OCR backends, GPU adapters, and supporting DLLs.

tools.name: vision_ocr_fast
tools.ability: Cached OCR entry point with backend metadata; current source does not yet prove the planned ONNX speedup.
tools.replacement: vision_ocr with automatic cache and backend metadata

tools.name: vision_screenshot
tools.ability: [Vision] Take a screenshot of the screen or a specific region. Returns the image path and optionally base64 data.

tools.name: vision_screenshot_hidden_window
tools.ability: Capture a window's content using the Win32 PrintWindow API, even when the window is obscured by other windows or off-screen. Does NOT raise/focus the window. Returns OCR text + saves the image. This is the canonical replacement for `window_screenshot(behind=true)` (renamed 2026-05-15 to surface the unique capability).

tools.name: vision_screenshot_ocr
tools.ability: [Vision] Take screenshot and immediately OCR it. Returns extracted text. Local processing.

tools.name: vision_zoom
tools.ability: Crop and magnify a screen region for closer inspection. Takes a screenshot, crops to the specified rectangle, and scales up.

tools.name: uia_app_launch
tools.ability: Launch an app by name using ShellExecuteW with a Start-menu search fallback.

tools.name: uia_batch
tools.ability: Execute multiple UIA (desktop) actions in sequence. Each action calls the same internal function as the individual tool. Stops on first error unless continue_on_error=true. Returns array of results.

tools.name: uia_click
tools.ability: Click by cached element_ref from the last uia_get_state snapshot, or fall back to exact screen coordinates.

tools.name: uia_find
tools.ability: Find UI elements by name or control type. Returns exact coordinates.

tools.name: uia_focus_window
tools.ability: Bring a window to the foreground by title substring match.

tools.name: uia_get_state
tools.ability: Get the foreground window's desktop accessibility tree. Each returned node gets a stable ref ID (for example 'ref_12') that can be passed to uia_click or uia_type as element_ref. By default this only returns interactive elements to reduce token usage.

tools.name: uia_hold_key
tools.ability: Press a key or key combination and hold it for N milliseconds before releasing. Useful for games, accessibility tools, or any app that distinguishes tap from hold. Same key syntax as uia_key_press. Duration capped at 60000ms (60s).

tools.name: uia_key_press
tools.ability: Press a key or key combination. Supports modifiers (ctrl, shift, alt, win) and named keys (enter, tab, f1-f12, etc). Examples: 'ctrl+s', 'alt+f4', 'ctrl+shift+p', 'enter', 'f5'.

tools.name: uia_list_window
tools.ability: List all visible windows with their positions and handles.

tools.name: uia_poll_event
tools.ability: Drain queued events from active watches. Returns events and removes them from the queue. Call periodically or on-demand to check what happened.

tools.name: uia_read_value
tools.ability: Read the text/value content of UI elements. Uses UIA ValuePattern and TextPattern to extract actual text from Edit fields, text boxes, documents, etc. Filter by name, automation_id, or control_type.

tools.name: uia_scroll
tools.ability: Scroll via mouse wheel at current cursor position or at specific coordinates. Supports vertical (up/down) and horizontal (left/right).

tools.name: uia_shortcut
tools.ability: Execute a named shortcut for a known app. Pass no args to list apps. Pass app only to list its shortcuts. Pass app+action to execute. Supports: vscode, cursor, chrome, explorer, windows, notepad, terminal.

tools.name: uia_type
tools.ability: Type text into a cached element_ref from the last uia_get_state snapshot, or into the currently focused element.

tools.name: uia_watch
tools.ability: Register a watch for desktop events. Background thread polls every 750ms and queues events. Use uia_poll_events to drain. Types: 'focus' (window focus changes), 'window_list' (windows open/close), 'element_value' (UI element text changes). Actions: 'add', 'remove', 'list', 'clear'.

tools.name: uia_window_move
tools.ability: Move a window to screen coordinates by title substring match.

tools.name: uia_window_resize
tools.ability: Resize a window by title substring match.

tools.name: uia_window_snap
tools.ability: Snap a window to a screen region by title match.

tools.name: uia_window_state
tools.ability: Change window state by title match: minimize, maximize, restore, or close.

tools.name: hands_monitor_scope
tools.ability: List displays and set, inspect, or clear the central fail-closed monitor boundary used by visual, UIA, and coordinate actions.
